news.summary: A popular sandwich chain, Capriotti's Sandwich Shop, recognized for its delicious cheesesteaks and turkey subs, is expanding its presence in the Youngstown area. The chain will be holding a grand opening on April 29th in Niles, Ohio. Capriotti's is known for its hand-crafted sandwiches and has garnered acclaim for its offerings. The opening of the new location signifies the brand's commitment to growth and serving more customers in the community.
news.headline: Capriotti's Sandwich Shop franchisee of the year opens new location in Niles, Ohio on April 29th, expanding the chain's reach.

news.summary: In Pittsburgh on April 17, 2025, an inventor from Columbus, Ohio, shared plans for GY Lighters, a new invention aimed at helping smokers keep track of their lighters. The inventor explained that the idea originated from a desire to address the issue of losing lighters. The innovative design of GY Lighters includes an alert system that notifies the user if they have left the lighter behind. This feature is intended to provide a solution to the common problem of misplacing lighters.
news.headline: Inventor from Columbus creates G Y LIGHTERS to prevent loss with alert system. (CLM-719)

news.summary: On April 17, 2025, it was announced that the North Riyadh Geopark and Salma Geopark in Saudi Arabia have been recognized by UNESCO as members of the Global Geoparks Network. This recognition signifies a crucial step for Saudi Arabia in conserving geoheritage and advancing sustainable development. The designation highlights the importance of these geoparks in the region and demonstrates Saudi Arabia's commitment to safeguarding natural sites. This move positions the two geoparks to benefit from international cooperation and support for their conservation efforts.
news.headline: North Riyadh Geopark and Salma Geopark from Saudi Arabia joined UNESCO's Global Geoparks Network, enhancing geoheritage preservation.

news.summary: A Software-as-a-Service provider, KDAN (TPEx: 7737), based in Irvine, California, revealed a significant enhancement to their primary PDF Software Development Kit (SDK), ComPDF, on April 17, 2025. The update integrates advanced AI features, catering to enterprise-level document management needs.
news.headline: KDAN (TPEx: 7737) upgrades PDF SDK technology, partners with distributors to expand; adds AI to ComPDF for document mgmt.

news.summary: Shares of UnitedHealth Group Inc. (NYSE: UNH) plummeted over 21% on Thursday, marking the stock's biggest decline since 1998. The drop came as the company announced disappointing quarterly results attributed in part to the escalating healthcare costs in the U.S. CEO Andrew Witty elaborated on the earnings call. Notably, UnitedHealth revised its fiscal 2025 earnings outlook, now anticipating adjusted earnings to fall within $26 to $26.50 per share compared to the previous $29.50 to $30 per share. This adjustment was influenced by increased care activity within UnitedHealthcare's Medicare Advantage sector, leading to higher utilization rates of physician and outpatient services. For further details, visit Benzinga.com.
news.headline: UnitedHealth Group CEO warns high US healthcare costs unsustainable, stock down 21%, lowers earnings guidance to $26-26.50 per share from earlier $29.50-30.

news.summary: In RENO, Nev., on April 17, 2025, CIQ unveiled a user-friendly management interface for the Warewulf cluster provisioning system. The new Management UI aims to simplify cluster administration, while an API has been developed to enable automation and integration at scale. This tech preview release highlights CIQ's commitment to enhancing user experience in cluster management. These advancements are crucial for organizations looking to efficiently handle the management of clusters. Such innovations are key in streamlining processes and facilitating scalability in cluster operations.
news.headline: CIQ unveils tech preview of new Warewulf Management UI and Open Source API to streamline HPC cluster management.

news.summary: Freddie Mac, based in McLean, Va., reported today that the 30-year fixed-rate mortgage averaged 6.83% according to its Primary Mortgage Market Survey. This rate, although slightly higher, has stayed below the 7% mark for thirteen straight weeks. Sam Khater, Freddie Mac's Chief Economist, commented on the data, noting that a year ago, rates had reached 7.1% with purchase application demand 13% lower than the current levels. More details can be found on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Freddie Mac reports that the 30-year fixed-rate mortgage now averages 6.83%, below 7% for the 13th consecutive week.

news.summary: Algorithmic trading strategies, commonly used in cryptocurrency trading, offer automation and efficiency. Strategies like trend following, arbitrage, market making, and mean reversion allow traders to react to price movements swiftly and handle large volumes. Execution algorithms, such as TWAP (time-weighted average price) and VWAP (volume-weighted average price), focus on discreetly entering or exiting positions. TWAP splits large orders evenly over time, ideal for quiet trades in low-liquidity markets, while VWAP factors in volume traded at each price to provide a more accurate average reflecting market value. Calculating TWAP and VWAP involves summing up prices at regular intervals and dividing by the number of intervals, but VWAP also considers trading volume. TWAP is suited for steady trades and quiet markets, while VWAP is useful for benchmarking against market activity. Real-world examples include a $250-million Bitcoin purchase by Strategy using TWAP to minimize slippage and a crypto VC firm's improved execution using TWAP for Instadapp. Kraken Pro incorporates VWAP as a technical indicator for precise and informed crypto trading decisions. Understanding the differences and applications of TWAP and VWAP can provide traders an edge in the cryptocurrency market.
news.headline: Explore differences between TWAP (ignores volume) and VWAP (considers volume) in crypto trading for better execution strategies.

news.summary: On April 17, 2025, in Stamford, Connecticut, Star Group, L.P. revealed an increase in its quarterly distribution for the period ending March 31, 2025. The distribution surged to $0.1850 per common unit, up from $0.1725 per common unit. Annually, this marks a $0.05 increase to $0.74 per unit. This uptick signifies the 13th consecutive year of annual dividend growth for the company, a renowned home energy distributor and services provider trading on the NYSE under the ticker symbol SGU.
news.headline: Star Group, L.P. raises quarterly distribution to $0.1850 per unit, annual distribution up by 5 cents to $0.74 per unit.

news.summary: Rio Tinto Plc (NYSE: RIO) has announced a collaboration with AMG Metals & Materials to potentially create a significant, environmentally-friendly aluminium project in India. The aim is to utilize renewable energy like wind and solar, along with pumped hydro storage, to run the plant. The partnership will evaluate the possibility of constructing a combined facility capable of producing up to 1 million tonnes per year (Mtpa) of primary aluminium and 2 Mtpa of alumina. The initial phase of the exploration will concentrate on... Further details can be found on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Rio Tinto teams up with AMG Metals & Materials for a green aluminium project in India. The project aims to use renewable energy and has a capacity for up to 1M tonnes per annum of primary aluminium and 2M tonnes per annum of alumina.

news.summary: Express Elevator recently completed the acquisition of Ascend Elevator on April 4, 2025, based in Brown Deer, Wisconsin. This move signifies a significant step forward in Express Elevator's expansion plans as it takes over the operations of the independent elevator service provider. The acquisition represents a strategic decision to enhance Express Elevator's market presence and service offerings. This development marks a notable achievement for Express Elevator and underscores its commitment to growth in the industry.
news.headline: Express Elevator acquires Ascend Elevator, expanding in Wisconsin. Acquisition completed on April 4, 2025.

news.summary: Coinbase Global Inc (NASDAQ: COIN) released a warning about a potential "crypto winter," as it gears up to report its Q1 earnings on May 8. The company started the year with strong momentum after positive remarks on crypto by the Trump administration but now faces challenges due to "sharply rising macro concerns," per Rosenblatt Securities. Analyst Chris Brendler has maintained a Buy rating for Coinbase Global but has revised the price target from $305 to $290. Q1 estimates have been adjusted downwards to account for a continued decrease in trading activity. More details can be found on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Coinbase Global warns of "crypto winter", Analyst lowers Q1 estimates; target reduced to $290 from $305.

news.summary: Bitcoin mining company Bit Digital has diversified its business strategy by acquiring an industrial building in Madison, North Carolina for $53.2 million through its Canadian subsidiary Enovum Data Centers Corp. The transaction includes a $2.25 million initial deposit, $1.2 million non-refundable. The acquisition was disclosed in a Form 8-K filed with the SEC and is expected to close on May 15. Bit Digital has also announced a new Tier 3 data center in Quebec, Canada, investing $40 million in upgrades. The company's CEO, Sam Tabar, stated that the Quebec operation is part of their strategy to deliver purpose-built AI infrastructure at scale. Amidst volatile crypto prices, mining firms are turning to AI data centers for higher revenue streams. Public Bitcoin miners sold over 40% of their BTC holdings in March, signaling economic pressure and the need to diversify revenue streams due to increasing costs. CoinShares report suggested that less profitable miners are shifting to AI and other workloads to stay viable. Cost per Bitcoin is a crucial metric as mining companies struggle to remain profitable post-halving. Public miners facing challenges in revenue are exploring alternative income sources.
news.headline: Bitcoin miner Bit Digital buys $53.2M facility for AI, HPC push. Also invests $40M in Quebec data center for AI operations. Miners diversify due to volatile market, with 40% selling holdings in March. Some shift to AI to stay profitable.
